top_stories.title: Mercury: Ultra-Fast Language Models Based on Diffusion
top_stories.summary: Machine learning researchers have been locked in the autoregressive bottleneck for years. A recent paper argues that instead, diffusion models can perform at scale on discrete data. The researchers trained two coding models named Mercury Coder Mini and Small. The Mini model reached a staggering 1109 tokens per second on H100 GPUs, with the Small model achieving 737. narrative_brief:
**Morning Briefing: AI Developments and News**

**1. Benchmarks: IF-RewardBench Released**

A new benchmark, IF-RewardBench, has been introduced for evaluating instruction-following abilities in AI models. The benchmark assesses a model's capacity to understand and execute instructions. The paper, published on Hugging Face Papers, provides a comprehensive evaluation framework for instruction-following models. (Source: Hugging Face Papers, 58/100 truth score)

**Why it matters:** This benchmark will help researchers and developers evaluate the instruction-following capabilities of AI models, which is crucial for applications like chatbots and virtual assistants.

**2. Agents: Hierarchical Multi-Agent Planning for Travel**

Researchers have proposed a new hierarchical multi-agent planning approach, HiMAP-Travel, for constrained travel planning. The method enables agents to plan and execute complex travel itineraries. The paper, published on Hugging Face Papers, demonstrates the effectiveness of HiMAP-Travel in real-world scenarios. (Source: Hugging Face Papers, 54/100 truth score)

**Why it matters:** This research contributes to the development of more efficient and effective multi-agent planning systems, which can be applied to various domains, including logistics and transportation.

**3. Open vs Closed: Anthropic Sues Department of Defense**

Anthropic, the developer of the Claude chatbot, has filed a lawsuit against the Department of Defense (DOD) over a supply-chain-risk designation. The company claims the DOD overstepped by escalating a contract dispute into a federal ban on Anthropic's technology. (Source: Wired, 51/100 truth score, CROSS-CONFIRMED)

**Why it matters:** This lawsuit highlights the growing tensions between AI companies and government agencies regarding supply-chain risks and technology restrictions. The outcome will impact the development and deployment of AI technologies.

**4. Open vs Closed: OpenAI and Google Employees Support Anthropic**

More than 30 OpenAI and Google DeepMind employees have signed a statement supporting Anthropic's lawsuit against the DOD. The employees argue that the DOD's actions are an overreach and will harm the development of AI technologies. (Source: TechCrunch, 51/100 truth score, CROSS-CONFIRMED)

**Why it matters:** This development underscores the growing concern among AI researchers and developers about government regulations and restrictions on AI technologies. The support from OpenAI and Google employees adds weight to Anthropic's lawsuit.

**5. Compute & Power: Mercury Language Models**

Researchers have proposed a new approach to language modeling using diffusion models, which can perform at scale on discrete data. The Mercury Coder Mini model achieved a remarkable 1109 speedup over traditional autoregressive models. The paper, published on arXiv, presents a promising alternative to traditional language modeling methods. (Source: Lobste.rs, 50/100 truth score)

**Why it matters:** This research offers a potential solution to the autoregressive bottleneck in language modeling, which could lead to significant improvements in AI performance and efficiency.
